title: Create requirements asset directory structure
description: Create docs/standards/hla-1516-2010/requirements with subdirectories for latest, catalogs, history, verification, dashboards, and manifests.
acceptance_criteria: Repository contains a stable requirements directory and README explaining asset purpose and provenance.

title: Place v1.0 catalog assets as canonical sources
description: Copy v1.0 master requirements, verification matrix, clause tracker, clause details, API catalogs, and summary into the repo as canonical requirements inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical CSV and XLSX artifacts are committed; manifest checksums match this packet.

title: Define requirements CSV schema contract
description: Codify expected columns and categorical values for requirements, verification matrix, clause tracker, and catalogs.
acceptance_criteria: A schema document exists and validation tests fail on missing columns, duplicate requirement_id values, invalid statuses, or orphaned verification rows.

title: Add requirements loader module
description: Implement a small loader that reads requirement CSVs into typed records for search, linting, documentation generation, and test traceability.
acceptance_criteria: Loader parses all canonical CSVs with zero errors and exposes records by requirement_id, standard, clause, capability, feature, and test_id.

title: Add requirements lint command
description: Create a CLI or make target that validates schema, uniqueness, referential integrity, checksum drift, and clause tracker coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Command exits nonzero on duplicate IDs, orphan test_id rows, missing clauses, unexpected columns, or stale manifest hashes.

title: Hook pytest tests to requirement IDs
description: Adopt pytest markers or metadata so implementation tests can reference requirement_id and verification matrix rows.
acceptance_criteria: At least Clause 4 lifecycle tests and Clause 5 declaration tests declare requirement links; CI report can list requirements covered by tests.

title: Add CI validation for requirements assets
description: Run requirements linting in CI before regular tests. Treat catalog corruption as a build failure.
acceptance_criteria: CI fails when the canonical requirements files are malformed or verification rows reference missing requirement IDs.

title: Generate human-readable requirements docs
description: Generate markdown pages grouped by standard, clause, capability, and service group from the master catalog.
acceptance_criteria: Docs can be regenerated from CSVs; generated files are either committed or produced in CI artifacts depending on repo policy.

title: Map requirements to implementation modules
description: Fill or refine implementation_area values to point to concrete packages/modules in the actual codebase.
acceptance_criteria: Each P0/P1 requirement has either a real module path or an explicit TODO/NotStarted marker.

title: Peer-review source clause traceability
description: Review exact clause and source_detail mappings against the standards PDFs/download artifacts, prioritizing Clause 4, Clause 5, Clause 6, Clause 8, and Clause 11.
acceptance_criteria: Reviewed rows are marked with a reviewed status or review note; unclear rows are flagged for refinement rather than treated as certified.

title: Create implementation work queues from requirements
description: Create issue backlog groupings for Federation Management, Declaration Management, Object Management, Ownership, Time, DDM, Support, MOM/MIM, OMT, XML, and transports.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog issues or project cards refer to requirement IDs and acceptance tests.

title: Decide handling of restricted standards inputs
description: Do not publish IEEE PDFs or downloaded standards artifacts to a public repo unless license terms permit. Keep them in a private artifact store or document how developers provide them locally.
acceptance_criteria: Repo policy is explicit: generated requirement catalogs may be committed; source standards are either excluded or stored only in approved private locations.
